footer.tagline: Preserving and teaching Ethiopian Orthodox spiritual music for the diaspora, one Kirar at a time.
footer.joinCommunityDescription: Lessons, new instruments, and gathering invitations — a few times a month.

hero.title: Learn the Kirar. Carry the tradition forward.
hero.description: Abelkirar trains the Ethiopian and Eritrean Orthodox diaspora to play the Kirar with skill and reverence — so worship sounds the way it was always meant to.

mission.title: Preserving Ethiopian spiritual music, one musician at a time
mission.paragraph1: Abelkirar exists to train Kirar players wherever the Ethiopian and Eritrean Orthodox diaspora has gathered — so that worship in churches across the United States, the United Kingdom, and Europe is carried by musicians who play with real skill and understanding.
mission.paragraph2: Abelkirar is led by a Kirar musician and Ethiopian Orthodox Church Deacon, teaching the instrument the way it has always been taught — by ear, by tradition, and in service of the liturgy — combined with structured, modern online lessons.
mission.quote: “Music that carries the liturgy shouldn’t be left to chance.”

home.coursesTitle: A curriculum built for every stage
home.coursesDescription: From your first scale to leading worship, structured for the Ethiopian and Eritrean diaspora — kids and adults alike.
home.instrumentsTitle: Instruments built for worship, made to order
home.instrumentsDescription: Kirar, Begena, and Masenqo, customized in shape, finish, and size, and shipped to the US, UK, and Europe.

beginner.title: Beginner
beginner.description: Build the foundation: how the Kirar is held, tuned, and played, and how it carries Ethiopian Orthodox worship.

intermediate.title: Intermediate
intermediate.description: Sharpen technique and rhythm, and begin playing alongside singers with a tone and style of your own.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Professional technique, complex rhythm and arrangement, and the performance skills to lead in church.

community.title: A community for Kirar players, wherever they live
community.description: Monthly online gatherings, student performances, and workshops with guest Ethiopian musicians — built for a diaspora scattered across continents but united in worship.

store.title: Handmade instruments, built for worship
store.description: Every instrument is made to order and customizable in shape, finish, and size. We ship to the United States, United Kingdom, and Europe.
store.empty: New instruments are being added soon — check back shortly.

about.title: A Deacon, a Kirar player, and a tradition worth carrying forward
about.paragraph1: Abelkirar is led by an Ethiopian Orthodox Church Deacon and Kirar musician.
about.paragraph2: Serving the church revealed a gap that shaped Abelkirar’s mission: too many congregations, especially in the diaspora, lack musicians trained to play the Kirar well. Hymns that should lift a service often fall flat — not from a lack of devotion, but from a lack of structured teaching.
about.paragraph3: Abelkirar was built to close that gap: rigorous, structured Kirar education, delivered online, for Ethiopian and Eritrean Orthodox communities wherever they’ve settled — from parish halls in Addis Ababa to living rooms in the United States and United Kingdom.
about.whyTitle: Preservation is an active choice, not a memory
about.whyDescription: Ethiopian Orthodox spiritual music has survived centuries. It survives the next century only if it's taught deliberately — to the diaspora, to the next generation, and to anyone drawn to it.
about.preserveDescription: Keep the traditional playing techniques, scales, and church repertoire intact and accurately taught.
about.teachDescription: Structured, level-based online courses that take a student from silence to leading worship.
about.modernizeDescription: Bring the Kirar to a global, digitally-connected diaspora without losing what makes it sacred.

newsletterWelcome.subject: Welcome to Abelkirar
newsletterWelcome.body: Thank you for joining the Abelkirar community. You'll hear from us with Kirar lessons, new instruments, and community events.
